complexity_factors: Bedrock model access no longer requires manual console enablement — Amazon models (Nova Canvas, Titan V2) auto-enable on first invocation; Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6 requires aws-marketplace:Subscribe in IAM for auto-enablement
Pillow text overlay coordinate tuning per aspect ratio requires iterative refinement â€” AI-assisted but may need multiple passes
Claude Code skill scaffolding (8 skills + hooks + .mcp.json) adds scope vs. v1.0 CLI approach, offset by lower per-skill complexity
GitHub Actions CI/CD with OIDC or Secrets configuration adds new scope vs. v1.0 (was out-of-scope)

All development hours are AI-assisted (Claude Code + Sonnet) â€” not raw human labor. 3-5Ã— multiplier over manual for boilerplate; ~1Ã— for novel problem-solving (Pillow coordinates, prompt engineering)

novel_technology_stack.note: Bedrock image generation (Nova Canvas) and AnthropicBedrock text client are relatively new API patterns

multiple_integrations.note: 3 integrations: S3 + Bedrock image generation + Bedrock text reasoning â€” just above 2-integration baseline

high_performance_requirements.note: P95 60s/image target, 1 concurrent user â€” trivially achievable

data_pipeline_complexity.note: Linear pipeline with clear inputs/outputs â€” no branching, streaming, or fan-out

unfamiliar_tech_stack.note: Claude Code custom skills scaffold plus AnthropicBedrock client are new additions vs. v1.0

evolving_requirements.note: Well-defined PoC scope with explicit acceptance criteria â€” requirements locked at v2.0

critical_external_dependencies.note: Bedrock console enablement retired — Amazon models auto-enable on first invocation. Claude Sonnet 4.6 needs aws-marketplace:Subscribe in IAM policy (one-line addition). G-001 is now a first-invocation verify, not a blocking pre-start console step.

security_compliance_requirements.note: IAM + SSE-S3 + S3 Block Public Access â€” no regulated compliance frameworks for PoC

real_time_processing.note: Batch skill-invoked execution â€” not real-time or event-driven

complex_business_logic.note: Straightforward pipeline â€” no conditional domain-specific rules beyond model tier selection

by_phase.notes: AI-assisted. Repo creation, directory structure, CloudFormation (S3 + IAM + Budget alarm), venv + requirements.txt, CLAUDE.md, 8 Claude Code skill stubs (.claude/skills/), .claude/settings.json hooks, .mcp.json AWS MCP servers, initial commit + push. Human gates (Bedrock console, AWS configure) are not in these hours.

AI-assisted. C-002 SKU Parser, C-003 Asset Manager, C-004 Prompt Constructor, C-005 Image Generator (dev tier, 1:1 ratio, retry/backoff), C-006 Text Overlay (1:1 layout), C-007 Output Manager + manifest, C-012 Text Reasoning Engine (AnthropicBedrock). /run-pipeline skill invokes pipeline. Minimum demo state after this phase.

AI-assisted. Extend C-005 to all 3 model tiers + image caching (~/.cache/pai-pipeline/). Extend C-006 to all 3 aspect ratios (1:1, 9:16, 16:9) with correct layouts. --dry-run mode end-to-end. Full run produces 6+ images (2 products Ã— 3 ratios). /pipeline-status and /view-results skills functional.

AI-assisted. GitHub Actions ci.yml (ruff lint + pytest unit tests + pip-audit on push), deploy.yml (CloudFormation deploy on main). Pytest test suite: unit tests for all 5 pipeline modules + integration test (dry-run). ruff/flake8 linting. Verify PostToolUse auto-lint and Stop test gate hooks.

AI-assisted. README.md (setup, how-to-run, example I/O, design decisions, assumptions). BACKLOG.md (future roadmap). 4 demo SKU briefs in inputs/demo_briefs/. /generate-demo skill produces full demo dataset. demo-script.md. Final git tag v1.0.0.

by_component.component: C-002 SKU Brief Parser + JSON schema validation
C-006 Text Overlay Engine (Pillow, 3 aspect ratios)
C-007 Output Manager (S3 + flat JSON manifest)
C-008 IaC Stack (CloudFormation: S3 + IAM + Budget)

risk_buffer.rationale: Medium complexity (4/10): G-001 first-invocation verify (low risk — auto-enablement), Pillow coordinate tuning for 3 aspect ratios, and potential GitHub Actions OIDC/credential setup issues justify 25% buffer over PERT baseline of 9.83h. AI-assisted development partially offsets this with faster iteration cycles.

three_point_detail.note: Optimistic: AI completes pipeline first-pass, Bedrock auto-enables on first invocation (no wait), no Pillow iteration needed. Most likely: typical AI-assisted session with 1-2 Pillow coordinate passes and one CI/CD auth issue. Pessimistic: IAM marketplace permission missing from policy (10-min fix), Pillow text wrapping requires 3+ passes per ratio, GitHub Actions OIDC setup requires troubleshooting.

responsibilities: All 12 components (C-001 through C-012): implementation, testing, documentation
8 Claude Code skill files and settings.json hooks
Test suite (pytest), README, BACKLOG, demo data

team_composition.role: Paul Praeducer â€” Human Architect / Decision Maker
responsibilities: HUMAN ACTION: Add aws-marketplace:Subscribe/Unsubscribe/ViewSubscriptions to IAM user policy (Claude Sonnet 4.6 auto-enablement); run aws configure --profile pai-exercise; approve visual quality gates G-002 and G-003
HUMAN GATE: Run aws configure --profile pai-exercise and verify sts get-caller-identity
HUMAN GATE: Verify gh auth status, create GitHub repo praeducer/pai-take-home-exercise
Architecture decision approval and approve/continue gates between phases
Human inspection of generated images at Phase 2 completion (minimum demo state gate)
Human review of CI/CD passing checkmarks before Phase 5

development.note: Take-home interview exercise â€” developer time is candidate's own. Professional rate reference: 10-12 AI-assisted hours Ã— $150-200/hr SA rate = $1,500-2,400 if engaged commercially.

cost_model.infrastructure_monthly_notes: S3 storage for PoC outputs (~100MB) is negligible ($0.023/GB â†’ <$0.01/month). CloudFormation is free. No compute infrastructure (no Lambda, no EC2). Budget is unconstrained â€” optimize for quality and speed, not cost minimization.
cost_model.third_party_monthly_notes: Per-run costs: Nova Canvas (final tier) $0.04/image Ã— 12 images = $0.48/run (source: aws.amazon.com/nova/pricing/). Titan V2 (dev tier) $0.01/image Ã— 12 = $0.12/run (source: aws.amazon.com/bedrock/pricing/). Claude Sonnet 4.6 text reasoning ~1K tokens/run Ã— $3/MTok input = ~$0.003/run. Total expected for PoC: 10 dev runs + 5 final runs + 4 demo SKU briefs at final tier = ~$5 total.

genai_per_run.note: Per-run cost at 3 aspect ratios Ã— 2 products Ã— 2 images each (2 products = 12 images total per run). Budget unconstrained â€” quality over cost. Text reasoning cost is negligible at PoC scale.

tco_3_year.note: PoC has no ongoing maintenance cost after interview. Production evolution would add: S3 storage at scale, CloudWatch Logs retention, Bedrock production traffic at $0.04-0.48/packaging set depending on tier.

roi.note: ROI not quantified for interview PoC. Production reference: manual packaging design ~2-4 hours/SKU variant at $50-100/hr agency rate. At 100 variants/month = $10,000-40,000/month manual cost. GenAI automation at $0.04-0.48/variant = $4-48/month â€” 99%+ cost reduction per variant, pending production validation.

three_pass_context.estimation_basis: Post-architecture v2.0 + security review v2.0 + data model v2.0. Component breakdown is well-understood. Estimates reflect AI-assisted development (Claude Code + Sonnet), not raw human labor. External dependency (Bedrock model access) remains the primary schedule risk.
human_action_list: Add aws-marketplace:Subscribe, aws-marketplace:Unsubscribe, aws-marketplace:ViewSubscriptions to IAM user policy — required for Claude Sonnet 4.6 first-invocation auto-enablement. Amazon models (Nova Canvas, Titan V2) need no action. ~1 minute.
Run: aws configure --profile pai-exercise (enter Access Key ID, Secret, region=us-east-1, output=json). Verify: aws sts get-caller-identity --profile pai-exercise returns account 730007904340.
Verify: gh auth status shows authenticated as praeducer. If not: gh auth login --web.
Create GitHub repo: gh repo create praeducer/pai-take-home-exercise --public --clone --gitignore Python
After Phase 3: Human inspection of 6+ generated images â€” confirm text overlay readable across all 3 aspect ratios (this is the G-002/G-003 gate and minimum demo state approval).
After Phase 4: Check GitHub Actions tab â€” confirm ci.yml and deploy.yml show green checkmarks on main branch (G-004 gate).
caveats: All hours are AI-assisted wall-clock time â€” Claude Code executes at roughly 3-5Ã— human speed for boilerplate, approximately equal for novel problem-solving
Human gate resolution time (Bedrock access, AWS configure) is not included in AI-assisted hours â€” it runs in parallel
Pillow text overlay is the highest-variance task â€” coordinate mapping for 3 aspect ratios may require 2-3 AI iteration passes
GitHub Actions OIDC setup requires creating an Identity Provider in IAM console â€” this adds ~15 minutes human time if OIDC is chosen over GitHub Secrets
Bedrock pricing verified against public pricing pages as of 2026-03; prices may change at any time

optimization_strategies.strategy: Use Titan V2 (dev tier) during all development iterations, Nova Canvas only for demo
optimization_strategies.savings: 75% cost reduction per run ($0.12 vs $0.48 for 12 images)
optimization_strategies.trade_off: Lower visual quality during development â€” acceptable since Pillow coordinate debugging does not require production-quality images

optimization_strategies.strategy: Image caching in ~/.cache/pai-pipeline/ keyed by content hash
optimization_strategies.savings: Eliminates duplicate Bedrock calls when iterating on text overlay â€” each re-run after a Pillow change costs $0 for images already generated
optimization_strategies.trade_off: Requires local disk space (~5-10MB per run)

optimization_strategies.strategy: --dry-run mode for all pipeline testing and debugging
optimization_strategies.savings: Zero GenAI cost â€” validates SKU brief parsing, prompt construction, and S3 manifest without Bedrock invocations
optimization_strategies.trade_off: Does not validate actual image quality â€” must switch to real run before Phase 3 gate

optimization_strategies.strategy: AWS Budget alarm at $25 with email notification
optimization_strategies.savings: Prevents surprise charges if caching breaks or --dry-run flag is accidentally omitted in a loop

assumptions: All development hours are AI-assisted (Claude Code + Sonnet) â€” not raw human labor hours
Paul's active decision-making time (approvals, AWS console, git) is ~20% of total session time

Amazon Bedrock serverless models in us-east-1 auto-enable on first invocation — no console enablement step required. Anthropic models (Claude Sonnet 4.6) require aws-marketplace:Subscribe in IAM for auto-enablement. Amazon, DeepSeek, Mistral, Meta, and Qwen models do not require marketplace permissions.
Nova Canvas pricing: $0.04/standard quality image at 1024Ã—1024 (source: aws.amazon.com/nova/pricing/)
Titan Image Generator V2 pricing: $0.01/image at 1024Ã—1024 (source: aws.amazon.com/bedrock/pricing/)
Claude Sonnet 4.6 via Bedrock: $3.00/MTok input tokens (source: aws.amazon.com/bedrock/pricing/)
Budget is unconstrained â€” quality and development speed take priority over cost minimization
No third-party licenses required â€” Python, boto3, anthropic, Pillow, pytest, ruff are all open source
Developer has local Python 3.12+ environment, uv (Python package manager for MCP server execution), and AWS CLI 2.x+. Node.js is NOT required â€” AWS MCP servers run via uv tool run, not npx.
GitHub account praeducer exists and gh CLI is authenticated
PoC does not require production-quality packaging imagery â€” functional demonstration is sufficient
